description_long: This stone temple features a raised, stepped platform with a broad staircase leading to a central sanctum. The structure is surrounded by a perimeter wall and features a prominent square tower (shikhara) at its core, flanked by smaller shrines and open courtyards. The exterior is constructed from large, gray stone blocks with a tiled, sloped roof and a flat terrace area. The main entrance is marked by a wide stairway and a central pathway leading directly to the sanctum. Minimal ornamentation is visible, with the focus on geometric lines and the solidity of the stonework. The overall design suggests a traditional South Indian or Dravidian architectural influence, with emphasis on symmetry and elevation.
description: A stepped stone temple with a central tower, broad stairway, and perimeter walls, featuring a sloped tiled roof and minimal exterior ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main staircase, central pathway, and the prominent entrance leading to the sanctum.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sloped tiled roof, perimeter wall, and the central tower rising above the structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the central tower and the symmetry of the roof and walls.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the smaller shrine structure and the open courtyard area adjacent to the main sanctum.
attribute_ornamentation: minimalist with geometric stonework and sparse decorative elements
attribute_entrance_design: Wide, straight staircase leading to a rectangular entry framed by stone blocks, with no visible arch or elaborate ornamentation
